operating_point_name: Unseen Cell-Type or Condition Perturbation Split
deployment_question: How does the model predict perturbation response in a cell type, tissue, condition, or niche absent from training?
definition: Hold out cellular contexts while preserving or varying perturbation identities to evaluate context transfer in perturbation response prediction.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: sets same-context train overlap to zero while perturbation overlap may remain

implementation_recipe: Declare the context variable: cell type, tissue, condition, donor, or spatial niche.
Assign all observations from held-out contexts to test.
Stratify by perturbation overlap if testing context-only versus double-novel transfer.
quality_gates: Report which perturbations are shared between train and held-out contexts.
Measure baseline expression similarity between contexts.
failure_modes: Context holdout can be confounded with batch or dataset source.
Rare cell types can produce unstable metrics.
Shared perturbations may make the split easier than true context-and-perturbation novelty.

citations.title: PrePR-CT: Predicting Perturbation Responses in Unseen Cell Types
citations.evidence: Targets transcriptional response prediction in unseen cell types.

citations.title: A Systematic Comparison of Single-Cell Perturbation Response Prediction Models
citations.evidence: Reports no model consistently generalizes across cell types across perturbation datasets.
